question: What evidence supports preemptive TEVAR for uncomplicated acute or subacute type B aortic dissection?
source_access_type: Original publisher PDF held in private local source workspace
relevant_sections: 7.4.2. Management of Acute Type B Aortic Dissection
Recommendations for the Management of Acute Type B Aortic Dissection
Table 27. Consensus Features of Complicated Acute Type B Aortic Dissection
Table 28. High-Risk Features in Uncomplicated Acute Type B Aortic Dissection

extracted_claims.claim_text: In all patients with uncomplicated acute type B aortic dissection, medical therapy is recommended as the initial management strategy.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: In all patients with uncomplicated acute type B aortic dissection, medical therapy is recommended as the initial management strategy.
extracted_claims.section: 7.4.2. Management of Acute Type B Aortic Dissection
extracted_claims.table: Recommendations for the Management of Acute Type B Aortic Dissection
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: In patients with acute type B aortic dissection and rupture or other complications (Table 27), intervention is recommended.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: In patients with acute type B aortic dissection and rupture or other complications (Table 27), intervention is recommended.
extracted_claims.section: 7.4.2. Management of Acute Type B Aortic Dissection
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Recommendation 2; COR 1; LOE C-LD. This recommendation concerns complicated acute TBAD, not uncomplicated acute TBAD.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: In patients with rupture, in the presence of suitable anatomy, endovascular stent grafting, rather than open surgical repair, is recommended.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: In patients with rupture, in the presence of suitable anatomy, endovascular stent grafting, rather than open surgical repair, is recommended.
extracted_claims.section: 7.4.2. Management of Acute Type B Aortic Dissection
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Recommendation 2 subrecommendation for rupture; COR 1; LOE C-EO. This recommendation concerns rupture, not prophylactic treatment of uncomplicated TBAD.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: In patients with other complications, in the presence of suitable anatomy, the use of endovascular approaches, rather than open surgical repair, is reasonable.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: In patients with other complications, in the presence of suitable anatomy, the use of endovascular approaches, rather than open surgical repair, is reasonable.
extracted_claims.section: 7.4.2. Management of Acute Type B Aortic Dissection
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Recommendation 2 subrecommendation for complications other than rupture; COR 2a; LOE C-LD. This recommendation concerns complicated acute TBAD.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: In patients with uncomplicated acute type B aortic dissection who have high-risk anatomic features (Table 28), endovascular management may be considered.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: In patients with uncomplicated acute type B aortic dissection who have high-risk anatomic features (Table 28), endovascular management may be considered.
extracted_claims.section: 7.4.2. Management of Acute Type B Aortic Dissection
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Recommendation 3; COR 2b; LOE B-R. The recommendation says endovascular management may be considered and does not recommend it routinely for every patient with uncomplicated acute TBAD.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: Table 28 identifies imaging and clinical features associated with higher risk in otherwise uncomplicated acute type B aortic dissection.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: High-Risk Imaging Findings: Maximal aortic diameter >40 mm; False-lumen diameter >20-22 mm; Entry tear >10 mm; Entry tear on lesser curvature; Increase in total aortic diameter of >5 mm between serial imaging studies; Bloody pleural effusion; Imaging-only evidence of malperfusion. High-Risk Clinical Findings: Refractory hypertension despite >3 different classes of antihypertensive medications at maximal recommended or tolerated doses; Refractory pain persisting >12 h despite maximal recommended or tolerated doses; Need for readmission.
extracted_claims.section: 7.4.2. Management of Acute Type B Aortic Dissection
extracted_claims.table: Table 28. High-Risk Features in Uncomplicated Acute Type B Aortic Dissection
extracted_claims.source_location_note: The table separates high-risk imaging findings from high-risk clinical findings. Recommendation 3 specifically says high-risk anatomic features; specialist review should confirm how the clinical findings should be applied to that recommendation.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for transcription; interpretation pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: The guideline states that the ADSORB trial demonstrated differences in remodeling-related findings, while the primary clinical benefits remained unknown.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: TEVAR was superior to optimal medical management alone with significant differences in incomplete or no false-lumen thrombosis, aortic dilation, and rupture, but the primary clinical benefits are unknown.
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Guideline summary of ADSORB. False-lumen thrombosis and aortic dilation are remodeling/imaging findings and must not be treated as proof of a mortality benefit.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for guideline-text extraction; underlying trial remains the primary source for trial outcomes

extracted_claims.claim_text: The guideline states that INSTEAD-XL associated prophylactic TEVAR plus optimal medical therapy with improved 5-year aorta-specific survival and delayed disease progression in uncomplicated type B aortic dissection.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: In the INSTEAD-XL (Investigation of Stent-grafts in Aortic Dissection) trial, in patients with uncomplicated type B aortic dissection, prophylactic TEVAR plus optimal medical was associated with improved 5-year aorta-specific survival and delayed disease progression.
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Guideline summary of INSTEAD-XL. The original PDF wording is transcribed exactly and omits the word 'therapy' after 'optimal medical.' The sentence does not label the population acute; specialist review should preserve the distinction from the acute population in Recommendation 3 and consult the underlying trial for outcome details.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for guideline-text extraction; underlying trial remains the primary source for trial outcomes

extracted_claims.claim_text: The guideline states that larger trials with longer-term data are still needed and that the optimal timing for TEVAR remains unknown.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: As the long-term mortality rate for type B aortic dissection that is managed medically and is strongly related to aortic events, the findings from the ADSORB and INSTEAD-XL trials appear promising, but larger trials with longer-term data are still needed. What remains unknown is the optimal timing for TEVAR.
extracted_claims.source_location_note: This uncertainty statement follows the guideline's summaries of ADSORB and INSTEAD-XL.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; pending specialist review

guideline_recommendations.recommendation: In patients with rupture, in the presence of suitable anatomy, endovascular stent grafting, rather than open surgical repair, is recommended.

guideline_recommendations.location: Printed p. e411; PDF p. 78; Section 7.4.2

guideline_recommendations.recommendation: In patients with uncomplicated acute type B aortic dissection who have high-risk anatomic features (Table 28), endovascular management may be considered.
guideline_recommendations.population: Uncomplicated acute type B aortic dissection with high-risk anatomic features
guideline_recommendations.location: Printed p. e411; PDF p. 78; Section 7.4.2; Table 28

limitations: Recommendation 3 is limited to uncomplicated acute type B aortic dissection with high-risk anatomic features; it is not a recommendation for routine prophylactic TEVAR in every uncomplicated acute case.
Section 7.4.2 does not provide a separate recommendation for uncomplicated subacute or chronic type B aortic dissection.
The supportive text summarizes ADSORB and INSTEAD-XL, but those trial publications remain the primary sources for numerical outcomes and population definitions.
The guideline explicitly states that the primary clinical benefits in ADSORB were unknown and that larger trials with longer-term data were still needed.
The optimal timing for TEVAR is stated to be unknown.
reviewer_notes: Original-source extraction only. No item has been specialist validated, verified by a reviewer, or made suitable for generated answers. Table 27 complicated features and Recommendation 2 are retained as context so they are not conflated with uncomplicated acute TBAD. No numerical outcome record was created from the guideline's secondary summaries of trials.
